The Lyon star had undergone a medical with the Anfield club ahead of a £53 million switch until the results of his examinations forced Liverpool to reconsider.

Fekir has a history of knee issues and it’s been reported that Liverpool feared they wouldn’t go away.

Liverpool may well decide to go after a different player and there are plenty who could take their fancy.

Liverpool are leading the race for Stoke City attacker Xherdan Shaqiri, sources close to the player have told ESPN FC.

Shaqiri, 26, is set to leave Stoke this summer following their relegation from the Premier League and a number of clubs have been weighing up moves for the Switzerland winger.

Sources have told ESPN FC that Shaqiri's brother and agent met with Liverpool officials last Friday to discuss a possible move to Anfield and that talks are also ongoing with several other clubs -- though the player himself is currently away in Russia preparing for the World Cup.

Shaqiri is reported to have a release clause of £13.5 million in his contract and that makes him an attractive proposition at that price, with sources telling ESPN FC that Liverpool made their interest clear last week.

The former Bayern Munich and Inter Milan midfielder scored eight goals and provided seven assists for Stoke last season and he has previously indicated he would prefer to stay in England if he does end up leaving Stoke.

Liverpool boss Jurgen Klopp is reportedly keen to boost his options in attack next season, as he wants to ease the burden on the likes of Mohamed Salah, Sadio Mane and Roberto Firmino.

Liverpool have begun talks with representatives of Stoke star Xherdan Shaqiri.

The Swiss international has remained tight-lipped over a move to Anfield when quizzed after his country's 2-0 World Cup warm-up victory over Japan on Saturday.

"I can't tell you anything," said the former Bayern Munich star.

"My agents take care of my future. I'm focusing on the national team right now."

With Shaqiri's eyes firmly on the prize of Russia 2018, his team begun talks with Liverpool officials last Friday, Bleacher Report journalist Dean Jones reports (via Stoke Sentinel).

Reports suggest Shaqiri is available for a knockdown £13.5m as a result of Stoke's relegation from the Premier League, and the likes of Everton, West Ham, Spurs and Mark Hughes' Southampton are also said to be interested in a deal.

The World Cup winger arrived at Stoke in the summer of 2015 intent on reviving his career in the hope of eventually earning a move to a Champions League club, so it is ironic his dream could be about to come true as a result of Stoke's relegation.

Shaqiri scored eight goals and created seven more last term in his best season for the Potters, but was unable to save them from the drop.

He made clear last week that he would be leaving the Potters this summer, despite having a contract which runs until the summer of 2020.

He told Swiss newspaper Aargauer Zeitung: "It is no secret that I will leave the club.

"Everyone wants to compete with the best, that was one reason why I chose the Premier League, and that's why I want to take the next step now.

"I would love to stay in England. I think the league is perfect for me. It's the best league with the best players, but I do not want to exclude anything."

Liverpool will step up their efforts to land Xherdan Shaqiri when he returns from the World Cup, after receiving initial encouragement.

But the Stoke forward is NOT an alternative to Nabil Fekir.

Shaqiri is keen on an Anfield switch, and a deal now looks possible if Stoke are willing to let him go close to the figure the Reds understand is contained in a break clause in his contract.

There is some confusion over that figure, with initial suggestions pinning the sum at around £12million after his club were relegated.

Liverpool wouldn't pay much beyond that price, with boss Jurgen Klopp not seeing him as a marquee signings, but a quality alternative to his front three, the 26 year old offering the pace and eye for goal to provide more strength in depth.

The Reds will take a step back from the transfer market over the next few weeks as the World Cup gets underway, but Shaqiri is possible once his country Switzerland exit the competition.

Fekir remains a possibility too – despite suggestions the transfer is stone dead.

Liverpool have reportedly made a £57million move for Roma keeper Alisson - despite suggestions the move was dead.

The Brazil keeper hasn't been the Reds top choice, but Liverpool chiefs believe Atletico Madrid stopper Jan Oblak is simply unattainable.

Now Brazilian outfit Terra claims the Reds have offered big money for the 25-year-old, as they push to bring in a new No.1 this summer.

Alisson, who keeps Man City's Ederson out the Brazil team, wants his future decided before the World Cup gets underway.

"Let's see if something happens this week," Alisson told reporters following Brazil's 3-0 victory over Austia. "I hope it will be resolved, yes, before the World Cup, that's what I want, to be honest."

Liverpool will reportedly ramp up their efforts to sign Stoke City's Xherdan Shaqiri once his World Cup finishes - but the winger is not a direct replacement for Nabil Fekir.

Liverpool are also keen to recruit depth out wide as they look to ensure they have ample back-up for their front three of Mohamed Salah, Roberto Firmino and Sadio Mane - who plundered a combined 91 goals last term.

And according to the Daily Mirror’s David Maddock, Liverpool will look to wrap up a deal for Shaqiri - whose Stoke side were relegated from the Premier League last month.

But they will not make a formal approach until after the World Cup with Shaqiri representing Switzerland in Russia over the coming weeks.

Stoke are thought to be willing to let their star man go for a reasonable price and the Mirror say that Shaqiri is keen to make the move to Anfield.

It has been widely reported that Shaqiri has a relegation release clause in his Potters contract that allows him to exit the Potteries for a fee in the region of £12million.

The Mirror add that Liverpool are keen to keep the deal at a low sum with Shaqiri only seen as an alternative to Salah and Mane out wide as opposed to a first-choice option.

But 26-year-old Shaqiri, formerly of Bayern Munich, is viewed as a bargain and supplied eight goals and seven assists last season despite playing in a goal-shy Stoke side.

Liverpool have launched a sensational bid for Roma goalkeeper Alisson Becker, according to reports in Brazil.

The transfer saga appeared to be over before it really got going on Monday, as Sky Sports reported that the Reds were being priced out of the deal.

However, reports closer to Alisson's home suggest Jurgen Klopp is ready to splash out on the Roma shot-stopper.

Brazilian outlet Terra claim Liverpool have launched a R$285m offer for the 25-year-old, which works out at £57m.

The publication claims this offer has been accompanied by a promise from Klopp, who has told Alisson he would be the No 1 at Anfield.

Real Madrid are also interested in securing his signature.

However, with Los Blancos currently without a manager, Liverpool may be able to swoop in with a bid.

Alisson has admitted he wants to sort his future before the World Cup, which gets underway on Thursday.

Terra claim if the Serie A stopper has not agreed a deal before the tournament in Russia, he will wait until Brazil are eliminated before reopening negotiations.

Liverpool may have been given another option in their pursuit of attacking reinforcements for next season.

Sporting Lisbon winger Gelson Martins was among several names being considered last summer before the Reds completed their £36.9million move for Mohamed Salah from Roma.

And now Martins could be available on a free transfer following a remarkable series of events at his Portuguese club.

The 23-year-old is one of four players to have filed notice with the Portuguese Securities Market Commission on Monday they were breaking their contracts with Sporting.

Their move comes after a difficult few weeks in Lisbon, notably last month when 50 fans forced their way into the team's training camp and assaulted players and staff.

Das Bost, who suffered a head injury in the incident, William Carvalho and Bruno Fernandes have joined Martins in rescinding their contracts, citing “just cause” in their explanation.

Sporting coach Jorge Jesus, goalkeeper Rui Patricio and forward Daniel Podence have already quit the club.

Liverpool will continue to consider their goalkeeper options with Alisson Becker's future expected to lie elsewhere.

The Reds have been linked with the Roma shot-stopper constantly during the past six months as Jurgen Klopp looks to overhaul his goalkeeping department.

A report in Brazil on Monday evening claimed the Reds had made a 65million euro bid for the player.

That, though, is untrue, with there being no change in Liverpool's stance from earlier this year when they informed Alisson's representatives not to expect a bid this summer.

Roma have no intention of selling the Brazil international, nor has the player asked to be put up for sale.

However, the Serie A side are putting in place a contingency plan should receive an offer they would struggle to refuse, said to be in excess of 70 million euros.

Roma are determined not to allow Liverpool or any club pick up any of their players for what could be considered a cut-price fee having been chastened by the success of Mohamed Salah at Anfield, the Egyptian joining for a comparatively cheap £36.9m.

Liverpool have kept tabs on Alisson but have made no contact with Roma, with indications the Reds believe a deal for the Brazil international would prove difficult.

And in any case, it is understood Alisson would prefer a move to Real Madrid should the Spaniards, who are considering their own goalkeeping position, choose to meet the asking price.

Liverpool target Alisson insists he is "very happy" at Roma and has held no discussions over a potential departure from the Serie A club.

The Brazil goalkeeper has been the subject of intense transfer speculation in the lead up to the World Cup.

Jurgen Klopp's side and their Champions League conquerors Real Madrid are believed to be most interested in his signature, while Chelsea have also been linked with the former Internacional shot-stopper.

Alisson has previously spoken of his hope to have his club future resolved prior to kick-off in Russia and it appears increasingly likely that he will stay at Stadio Olimpico.

"I'm focusing on my work with Brazil. People are taking care of other issues regarding Roma. Whatever happens will be for the best," the 25-year-old told reporters.

"I try not to read the news so much. It helps not to read speculation. Nothing official has come my way. I have a contract with Roma.

"I'm very happy in Italian football, I'm very happy in Rome."

Alisson's current deal with the Giallorossi runs until 2021. He is expected to start Brazil's opening World Cup match ahead of Manchester City's Ederson against Switzerland on June 17.

當然，在利物浦落實追逐費基亞的時候，亦應該清楚這名法國中場的傷病史，得悉司職進攻中場的他，曾在15-16年球季迎來前膝十字韌帶的斷裂，因而已做好心理準備，但經過體檢後，紅軍才發現此子的膝蓋舊患，存在著較高的風險，或與費基亞當年的手術方式有關。

一般來說，面對著前膝十字韌帶斷裂的情況，醫學界慣以兩種的手術方式處理，一是自體移植，二是異體移植。顧名思義，自體移植和異體移植的主要分別，在於是否抽取傷者本身的身體組織，作為修補韌帶斷裂的主要硬件（以腿筋或髕骨組織為主），但根據骨科專家 Dr. David Geier 的醫學文章，原來接受異體移植的病人，再次遇上同類型受傷的風險，竟比起接受自體移植的患者高出4.69倍。Dr. David Geier 更表示，自體移植才是職業運動員處理前膝十字韌帶斷裂的首選。

按此推斷，費基亞有可能就是接受異體移植的少數，而一度扮演買方角色的利物浦，卻在這名法國中場的體檢順利完成後，才發現這樣的情況，畢竟手術方式向來不會對外公佈，只有球員、球會和醫院這三個持份者，才享有優先的知情權。若事實正是如此，那麼利物浦的放棄求購，絕對是情有可原。

http://football.fanpiece.com/m/liverpoolforeverred/c1321896.html

當然，在利物浦落實追逐費基亞的時候，亦應該清楚這名法國中場的傷病史，得悉司職進攻中場的他，曾在15-16年球季迎來前膝十字韌帶的斷裂，因而已做好心理準備，但經過體檢後，紅軍才發現此子的膝蓋舊患，存在著較高的風險，或與費基亞當年的手術方式有關。

一般來說，面對著前膝十字韌帶斷裂的情況，醫學界慣以兩種的手術方式處理，一是自體移植，二是異體移植。顧名思義，自體移植和異體移植的主要分別，在於是否抽取傷者本身的身體組織，作為修補韌帶斷裂的主要硬件（以腿筋或髕骨組織為主），但根據骨科專家 Dr. David Geier 的醫學文章，原來接受異體移植的病人，再次遇上同類型受傷的風險，竟比起接受自體移植的患者高出4.69倍。Dr. David Geier 更表示，自體移植才是職業運動員處理前膝十字韌帶斷裂的首選。

按此推斷，費基亞有可能就是接受異體移植的少數，而一度扮演買方角色的利物浦，卻在這名法國中場的體檢順利完成後，才發現這樣的情況，畢竟手術方式向來不會對外公佈，只有球員、球會和醫院這三個持份者，才享有優先的知情權。若事實正是如此，那麼利物浦的放棄求購，絕對是情有可原。

前交叉韌帶重建手術，用什麼材料比較好？

一、自體組織。主要是髕腱和膕繩肌：1、關節鏡下採用自體膕繩肌(HT)，不管患者年齡如何，所有希望恢復運動功能且有指征進行韌帶重建的患者都可以採用HT重建。但採用HT重建ACL還有一些特殊的適應徵：已採用自體BTB重建ACL但失敗者、有髕股關節疾病而欲避免加重症狀者、對術後的美觀要求較高者、因經常跪地工作（如地毯工、木匠等）而要避免膝前痛和跪地痛者，以及髕腱短、有損傷或有病變者，不適合採用BTB重建ACL，而更適合採用HT重建ACL。 2、採用BTB移植物重建ACL以往是ACL重建的黃金標準。此項技術的優勢在於移植物具有足夠的強度和剛度，研究顯示14mm～15mm寬的BTB移植物的平均強度相當於正常ACL的168%。此外，移植物兩端的骨塊使BTB移植物與骨隧道的癒合早而可靠。採用BTB重建ACL的缺點除了供區併發症外，最主要的是無法進行ACL的解剖重建。


缺點：要取自體組織，對患者膝關節的旋轉有一定影響。優點：取自體肌腱，費用便宜。肌腱強度較大，且存在腱骨癒合，通俗講法就是移植肌腱能夠長成自己的韌帶。



原文網址：https://kknews.cc/zh-hk/health/4e6prg.html
二、異體組織。主要是異體髕腱、跟腱或膕繩肌腱。關節鏡下應用異體髕腱(骨-腱-骨組織,BPTB)或膕繩肌腱或跟腱重建膝關節前交叉韌帶：目前的異體髕腱、跟腱或者膕繩肌腱比以前的處理工藝明顯提高，因此，排異性明顯減少。故術後反應更小。關節鏡下應用深低溫冷凍異體BPTB、跟腱或者膕繩肌移植重建ACL具有手術時間少，創傷小，恢復快，且不需取自體肌腱。對患者功能恢復具有非常非常的療效。


缺點：理論上存在排異可能，費用比較貴。優點：強度更大，且存在腱骨癒合，通俗講法就是移植肌腱能夠長成自己的韌帶。不需取自體肌腱，對患者膝關節的旋轉等無任何影響。



原文網址：https://kknews.cc/zh-hk/health/4e6prg.html

【now.com體育】已故費倫天拿隊長艾斯托利的最新驗屍報告指，艾斯托利在心臟停頓前曾心跳加速，最終死亡。

根據意大利各大傳媒報道，前費倫天拿隊長艾斯托利（Davide Astori）可能患有心臟病，但一直未被發現。最初在艾斯托利逝世的時候，有報告指他是因心律過慢，最終心跳停頓而死亡，在睡夢中暴斃。

不過，最新的驗屍報告指出，艾斯托利的死因是心跳過快，這意味著他死前可能已經醒過來，如果有人在他身邊，有機會獲救。現在有關當局正在調查這個問題是否可以及早診斷，避免悲劇發生。

31歲的艾斯托利於今年3月4日上午，被發現倒斃在酒店房間裡，估計前一晚已不幸身亡。費倫天拿其後宣佈成立基金，旨在協助艾斯托利的女兒Vittoria成長。

至於法國中場費基亞，亦有可能於世界盃閉幕後投身晏菲路，畢竟利物浦依然對此子抱有強烈興趣，只要里昂願意降低要價，甚至提出一些可讓紅軍滿足的新條件，一切皆有可能。

佳尼表示經過接近全季養傷報銷後，新一季要由頭再證明自己。

「季尾重返戰陣，由於球隊場場硬杖場場關鍵，要我即時重新投入比賽的確有D吃力，但能夠上陣對我黎講都重要，令我可以再次接觸番比賽及為新一季提早開始作好準備。我好努力操弗自己，過程中有所得著。」

「休季期間球會都會對每個球員有規管，我地都要遵守，由於我的確缺陣太耐，所以我必須加倍努力，追番失去咗嘅時間。近期我嘅狀態已經回復到頂峰，急不及待季前準備馬上開始，再次向大家展示我嘅實力，強勢回歸！」

「我記得阿諾由U23慢慢打上一隊，由操練情況而睇，佢的確潛力無窮，剛剛過去嘅球季佢得到上陣機會，亦好好把握上位成功，佢表現真係令人眼前一亮，好有自信，用波好好。經歷咗重重困難成為利記主力，假若佢喺世界盃得到上陣機會，我深信佢會大放異彩。」

「由我受傷後，我見到利記及英軍都持續進步緊，對我絕對有鼓舞作用，帶俾我動力。正如我之前所講，依家已經回復最好狀態，我將要再次證明俾大家睇我係得嘅！」

【now.com體育】雖然葡超勁旅士砵亭多番否認，但炒魷風波似乎明朗化，4名葡萄牙國腳集體宣佈已跟球會解約。

士砵亭在歐霸盃出局後，多名球員被指遭到炒魷魚，主席因怕惹禍上身，臨時叫停，但球員先發制人，葡萄牙國家隊門將魯爾柏迪斯奧在世界盃前已經回復自由身。葡萄牙中場祖奧馬里奧（Joao Mario）與3名國腳威廉卡維奴、謝臣馬田斯、波基斯費南迪斯集體表示，日前已與士砵亭達成解約共識，齊以自由身出戰世界盃。

「我們沒有任何交代，此事與國家隊無關，目前我們只集中備戰世界盃。」祖奧馬里奧拒絕交代詳情：「這些事情不宜在國家隊討論，世界盃之後自有分曉。」

2016年歐國盃冠軍葡萄牙將於周五展開世界盃之旅，小組首場比賽出戰西班牙。
